behaviors.description: Trim conversation history to the most recent 10 messages before sending to the LLM when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: Escalation trigger check: the behavior phrase is "trimmed to the most recent 10 messages." This is a local selection/ordering rule and does not itself require OS/network/subprocess or model judgment. Concrete miss at lower level: none (Unit is the floor). Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but the behavior text does not require a cross-module handoff failure; it specifies a deterministic trimming rule only.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure history-trimming function with histories of size 0, 10, and >10; assert exactly the last 10 messages are kept in original order and older messages are dropped.

behaviors.description: Generated response should answer the customer’s question, not be generic or off-topic.
behaviors.justification: Escalation trigger phrase: "addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply." This is output-quality judgment from the real LLM. Concrete miss at lower (Integration): a stubbed model can always return a canned on-topic answer, so the test passes while real model responses are generic/off-topic in production. Not Workflow because this behavior is about one generation quality decision, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real model with representative customer questions and history; score whether answers are question-relevant (not generic/off-topic) using predefined rubric/human-labeled set and fail below threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects an overly formal response for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Escalation trigger phrase: "correctly identifies when a response is too formal ... and triggers regeneration." Correctness depends on real language-style judgment (LLM/ML-like decision) plus the regeneration trigger. Concrete miss at lower (Integration): mocked tone-check outputs fixed labels, so misclassification of real formal/casual nuance is invisible. Rejection-gate for Workflow: behavior text requires one tone judgment leading to one regeneration trigger, not a broader multi-agentic pipeline outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model/tone-check path with casual-tier scenarios containing intentionally formal drafts; verify the system flags them and issues a regeneration request, and that non-formal drafts are not unnecessarily regenerated.

behaviors.description: Persist full exchange (user message, first response, regenerations) to DB with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Escalation trigger phrase: "persisted to the database with correct timestamps" and includes multiple records across turns/regenerations. This is module handoff/state consistency between chat flow and repository/DB mapping. Concrete miss at lower (Unit): repository mocks can miss schema/transaction/order bugs where regeneration entries are dropped or timestamps are written to wrong columns. Integration-brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess boundary (so not System), no model-output quality dependence (not Agentic), and no multi-agentic correctness requirement (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Run service + real local test DB (transactional) through a conversation with regeneration; assert all expected rows exist, linkage/order is correct, and timestamps are populated and monotonic per exchange.

behaviors.description: If regeneration is triggered, only the second response is delivered to the customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: Escalation trigger phrase: "when the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft." This requires sequencing across multiple agentic steps: initial generation, tone decision, regeneration, and final delivery gating. Concrete miss at lower (Agentic): a single-step agentic test can validate tone or generation quality but still miss race/order bugs where the first draft is briefly emitted before replacement. Integration-brake: behavior is explicitly orchestration/handoff across LLM-involved stages, so keeping Integration would under-test the stated failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end conversation flow with real model calls where tone check triggers; capture emitted customer-visible messages and assert only regenerated response is ever sent while rejected draft is retained only internally/logged.
